mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values indicate inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean 60-day forward return (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC is consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary conditions that increase borrowing costs for corporations, suppressing earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak consumer demand despite aggregate growth, leading to earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Periods with rising GDPC1 and falling RSAFS show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals pent-up demand fulfillment and capacity utilization improvements, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during post-shock recoveries fails to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below baseline.
